text: Abstract With the rapid development of smart cities, fire incidents that cause damage to properties and threaten the safety of citizens remain a major concern. Conventional fire detection techniques do not meet the needs of smart cities in terms of accuracy and speed. This paper proposes a fire detection method based on deep learning technology, namely smart fire detection system (SFDS) by utilizing YOLOv8. The experimental results show that the SFDS has higher accuracy, less false alarm rate and lower cost compared with traditional fire detection methods. The proposed system can also be applied to detect other objects of interest in smart cities, such as gas leakages and flooding. In addition, the whole framework of smart city is proposed, which consists of application layer, fog layer, cloud layer and IoT layer. By utilizing the Fog and Cloud computing along with IoT layer, the proposed algorithm in the system can quickly collect and process the data in real time, thus effectively reducing the risk of properties and casualties caused by fire accidents. The experimental results show that the proposed SFDS has achieved the state-of-the-art performance in terms of precision and recall, where the precision of all classes is as high as 97.1%. Finally, the proposed system can be applied to fire protection of public places, forest fire detection and intelligent security systems.
explanation: The abstract uses broad, high-level claims and buzzwords with limited specifics (no datasets or experimental setups), a pattern often seen in AI-generated text.
